title: ADJUTORIX Runtime Timeout Registry
description: Canonical machine-readable registry of timeout budgets, lease windows, freshness windows, retry backoff ceilings, heartbeat tolerances, and recovery deadlines across ADJUTORIX app, preload, main process, agent, CLI, scheduler, verify, replay, ledger, command execution, terminal, watcher, and release flows. This file defines what constitutes lateness, staleness, abandonment, and forced failure across all governed runtime surfaces.
invariants: Every consequential asynchronous boundary must have an explicit timeout or lease.
A timeout must map to a deterministic enforcement action, not merely a log message.
Freshness windows and execution timeouts must remain distinct even when they share units.
Recovery deadlines must be shorter than the ambiguity window they are intended to close.
Renderer-facing waits may be shorter than authoritative waits, but may not silently invent success or completion.

timeouts.title: Default RPC Request Timeout
timeouts.description: Maximum duration a synchronous governed RPC request may occupy the authoritative request path before timeout handling is enforced.

timeouts.title: Initial RPC Reconnect Backoff
timeouts.description: Initial reconnect backoff for agent/client reconnect loops before exponential growth applies.

timeouts.title: Maximum RPC Reconnect Backoff
timeouts.description: Upper bound for reconnect backoff between agent connectivity attempts.

timeouts.title: Agent Health Evaluation Interval
timeouts.description: Interval between internal health and readiness evaluations inside the governed agent.

timeouts.title: App Agent Health Probe Interval
timeouts.description: Interval between app-side health probes against the local agent surface.

timeouts.title: Scheduler Poll Interval
timeouts.description: Core scheduler loop interval for queue advancement, timeout checks, and recovery coordination.

timeouts.title: Job Heartbeat Interval
timeouts.description: Expected heartbeat emission interval for running jobs under authoritative scheduler control.

timeouts.title: Job Heartbeat Grace Window
timeouts.description: Additional tolerance beyond expected heartbeat interval before a running job is considered stalled.

timeouts.title: Default Job Timeout
timeouts.description: Default upper bound for long-running scheduler jobs unless a stricter phase-specific timeout applies.

timeouts.title: Verify Run Timeout
timeouts.description: Upper bound for an authoritative verification aggregation before the verify transaction fails.

timeouts.title: Mutation Guard Freshness Window
timeouts.description: Maximum age of mutation-guard verify evidence before it becomes non-authoritative for consequential mutation.

timeouts.title: Release Guard Freshness Window
timeouts.description: Maximum age of release-guard verify evidence before it may no longer authorize release promotion.

timeouts.title: Replay Run Timeout
timeouts.description: Upper bound for authoritative replay and lineage evaluation before forced failure.

timeouts.title: Replay Result Freshness Window
timeouts.description: Maximum age of replay evidence before apply/release consumers must require re-evaluation.

timeouts.title: Default Governed Command Timeout
timeouts.description: Default timeout for governed command execution when command-profile-specific limits do not override it.

timeouts.title: Command Termination Grace Period
timeouts.description: Grace window after timeout before a command is force-terminated and marked failed.

timeouts.title: Terminal Session Idle Lease
timeouts.description: Maximum idle duration before a governed terminal session is considered expired for retention purposes.

timeouts.title: Workspace Restore UI Patience Budget
timeouts.description: Maximum time the renderer waits before surfacing that workspace restore continues in the governed runtime path.

timeouts.title: Preload Bridge Request Timeout
timeouts.description: Maximum duration a preload bridge call may wait for a main-process response before surfacing a governed timeout error.

timeouts.title: Watcher Poll Interval
timeouts.description: Polling interval when native file watchers are unavailable or degraded.

timeouts.title: Watcher Event Coalescing Window
timeouts.description: Time window for merging watcher bursts into a single governed update batch.

timeouts.title: Diagnostics Collection Timeout
timeouts.description: Upper bound for diagnostics collection before partial results or degraded summaries must be emitted.

timeouts.title: Ledger Persistence Timeout
timeouts.description: Upper bound for persisting authoritative ledger edges and entries after execution or verification completes.

timeouts.title: Confirmation Freshness Window
timeouts.description: Maximum age of explicit confirmation before it must be considered stale for consequential actions.

timeouts.title: Recovery Decision Deadline
timeouts.description: Maximum duration allowed for crash-recovery decision making before forced failure or rollback must occur.

timeouts.title: Recovery Resume Lease
timeouts.description: Lease duration granted to a resumed transaction before it must heartbeat or be treated as abandoned again.

timeouts.title: Release Promotion Timeout
timeouts.description: Upper bound for governed release promotion including artifact checks, attestation writes, and publication gating.

timeouts.title: Release Publication Grace Period
timeouts.description: Grace window allowed for completing final publication writes after promotion reaches its terminal stage.

timeouts.title: Structured Log Flush Timeout
timeouts.description: Upper bound for flushing critical log events during shutdown, crash handling, or forced teardown.

timeouts.title: Notification Auto Hide Duration
timeouts.description: Duration non-blocking notifications remain visible before auto-hide in the renderer.

timeouts.title: Application Shutdown Grace Period
timeouts.description: Time budget for coordinated shutdown, including log flush, session persistence, and child-process termination.
